said.quote: Recent Dutch coalition programmes have referenced a headline target of roughly 100,000 new dwellings per year through 2030, with additional federal funding flagged for later in the decade. (Specific cabinet, verbatim wording, and source URL not independently verified for this build.)
said.attribution: Public 100,000/year housing target across recent coalition programmes (specific attribution demo)

shows.finding: CBS 82235NED reports 79,910 gross new dwellings (new-build + conversions) added in 2025, down from 82,204 in 2024 and 87,833 in 2023 — roughly 20% below the 100,000/year reference, with the three-year trend moving in the opposite direction.
shows.dataSource: CBS 82235NED (Voorraad woningen; standen en mutaties vanaf 1921), field Woningbouw_5

synthesis: The 100k/year figure is the widely-used public reference. The observed three-year trend (87.8k → 82.2k → 79.9k) is below and trending further below that reference. The specific coalition programme text is marked demo until re-verified; the CBS numbers on the right are live.
